title: MalReBay
text: World Health Organization () recommends Therapeutic Efficacy Studies (TES)monitor antimalarial drug effectiveness. TES, patients uncomplicated malaria treated followed several weeks assess whether infection clears remains suppressed. key challenge follow-distinguishing two causes recurrent parasitaemia: recrudescence: reappearance parasites original infection partial clearance. + reinfection: new infection separate mosquito bite. distinction, known molecular correction, relies comparing parasite genotypes initial infection subsequent recurrence. MalReBay R package implements Bayesian framework molecular correction. Rather making deterministic call, uses genotyping data patient’s initial infection (day 0) recurrence samples estimate probability recurrence due recrudescence versus reinfection. Compared rule-based methods, MalReBay explicitly accounts genotyping uncertainty, within-host diversity, local allele frequency information, providing principled robust classification. tutorial provides step--step guide main analysis workflow, loading data interpreting final results. Starting conceptual framework.

title: MalReBay
text: patient ii, binary variable RiR_i indicates recurrence cause: Ri=1R_i = 1 recrudescence Ri=0R_i = 0 reinfection.Bayesian inference updates prior beliefs RiR_i using observed genotyping data DiD_i, producing posterior probability P(Ri=1|Di,θ)P(R_i=1|D_i,\theta). three components model : Prior P(Ri)P(R_i) baseline belief recrudescence seeing data. Likelihood P(Di|Ri)P(D_i|R_i) probability observed allele patterns given hypothesis, accounting allele frequencies, within-host diversity, genotyping errors. Posterior P(Ri|Di)P(R_i|D_i) updated probability recurrence recrudescence combining priors data. Recurrences events classified recrudescence P(Ri|Di)P(R_i|D_i) exceeds threshold (e.g., 0.5). probabilities solved analytically posterior distribution closed form; therefore, MalReBay uses Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling approximate .

title: MalReBay
text: main MalReBay() function automates full pipeline: takes paired genotyping data day 0 day recurrence, combines model parameters (genotyping error rates, within-host diversity, allele frequencies), runs MCMC sampling, returns posterior estimates recurrence classifications summarised patient study level. following sections describe required input formats prepare data.

title: MalReBay
text: MalReBay accepts two types genotyping data: Length-polymorphic markers (e.g., microsatellites, MSP, GLURP). Amplicon sequencing data (haplotype). primary input Excel file containing genotyping data. input structure similar types, one key difference: length-polymorphic columns store fragment lengths base pairs, amplicon sequencing columns store haplotype labels.

title: MalReBay
text: Excel file contain: Sample.ID column uniquely identifies sample includes “Day 0” “Day recurrence”. day sampling (0 baseline, X recurrence). Note sometimes included Sample.ID column (e.g., BD21-002D0 BD21-002D42). Site column indicating geographical origin sample. Marker columns named like LocusName_AlleleNumber (e.g., 313_1, 313_2, 313_3, etc.), cell represents parasite clones fragment length observed base pairs. example expected input format length-polymorphic data:

title: MalReBay
text: input data similar structure length-polymorphic data, marker columns store haplotype labels 90 instead allele lengths. example expected input format amplicon sequencing data:

title: MalReBay
text: running classification analysis, exploring genotyping data strongly recommended. MalReBay provides visualizations tailored data type can help : Identify informative frequent markers population Assess data quality completeness Evaluate overall marker polymorphism richness data types share two core visualisation types, MOI violin plots diversity charts. MOI Violin Plots: Show distribution number distinct parasite clones per infection, providing insight infection complexity across samples. Allele Diversity Pie Charts: Display relative frequencies different alleles within marker, highlighting dominant alleles overall marker polymorphism.

title: MalReBay
text: section, provide step--step example using MalReBay package classify recurrent malaria infections length-polymorphic genotyping data. use example dataset included package, containing data Therapeutic Efficacy Study (TES) conducted Angola 2021. dataset includes 7 microsatellite markers; 313, 383, TA1, POLYA, PFPK2, 2490, TA109, genotyped 70 patients three study sites: Benguela, Lunda Sul Zaire. focus single site: Benguela.
code: # Load required libraries library(MalReBay) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(purrr) library(tidyr) library(here) library(kableExtra)

title: MalReBay
text: import example dataset using MalReBay:::import_data(). dataset includes late failure samples additional samples.
code: input_file_lp <- system.file("extdata", "Angola_2021_TES_7NMS.xlsx", package = "MalReBay") marker_file   <- system.file("extdata", "makers_details.xlsx", package = "MalReBay")  # Import and validate data imported_data <- MalReBay::import_data(   filepath = input_file_lp,    marker_filepath = marker_file ) #> INFO: Detected 'length_polymorphic' data format. #> Warning in MalReBay::import_data(filepath = input_file_lp, marker_filepath = #> marker_file): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> INFO: Using 7 markers: 313, 383, TA1, POLYA, PFPK2, 2490, TA109

title: MalReBay
text: output folder defined store results generated analysis. folder already exist, created automatically.
code: # Use a temporary directory  output_dir_lp <- file.path(tempdir(), "malrebay_results")  if (!dir.exists(output_dir_lp)) {    dir.create(output_dir_lp, recursive = TRUE) }  # Prepare data for descriptive plots genotypedata_lp <- dplyr::bind_rows(imported_data$late_failures, imported_data$additional)

title: MalReBay
text: step, generate summary statistics explore genetic characteristics data running Bayesian model. Two key measures considered: Multiplicity Infection (MOI): estimates number distinct parasite clones per marker sample, providing insight within-host diversity. Marker diversity: visualizes distribution allele sizes marker, helping assess level genetic variability study population. descriptive outputs give context interpreting molecular correction results can highlight potential sources genotyping uncertainty.

title: MalReBay
code: if (nrow(genotypedata_lp) > 0) {   first_site      <- unique(genotypedata_lp$Site)[1]   genotypedata_s1 <- dplyr::filter(genotypedata_lp, Site == first_site)    moi_plots <- MalReBay::plot_moi(     genotypedata  = genotypedata_s1,     output_folder = output_dir_lp   )   print(moi_plots[[first_site]]) }

title: MalReBay
code: if (nrow(genotypedata_lp) > 0) {   MalReBay::plot_markers_diversity(     genotypedata    = genotypedata_lp,     data_type       = "length_polymorphic",     marker_info     = imported_data$marker_info,     output_folder   = output_dir_lp   ) }

title: MalReBay
text: MCMC sampler several parameters control runtime convergence criteria. quick tutorial, use relaxed configuration. real analysis, use iterations (e.g., max_iterations 10000 ) stricter thresholds ensure robust convergence.

previous_headings: 6. Example of classification: length-polymorphic markers > Step 4: MCMC Configuration
title: MalReBay
text: iter: total number iterations per chain (including warmup). burn_in_frac: fraction iterations used warmup (typically 0.5). chains: number independent Markov chains run.. adapt_delta: (Stan-specific) Controls step size sampling. Increasing (e.g., 0.95) helps “divergent transitions.”

title: MalReBay
text: Now can run analysis. MalReBay() print progress messages console, informing data type detected, sites analyzing, status MCMC convergence. ‘future’ package used background parallel processing, can expect analysis run faster multi-core machines. MalReBay() function returns list containing two key data frames: summary: Main results patient, including posterior probability recrudescence number comparable loci. marker_details: Detailed information marker patient. Classification results showing posterior probabilities. Key columns summary include: Site: geographical site sample. Sample.ID: unique patient identifier. Probability: posterior probability infection recrudescence. value near 1.0 suggests recrudescence, value near 0.0 suggests reinfection. N_Available_D0 / N_Available_DF: number loci genetic data Day 0 Day Failure, respectively. N_Comparable_Loci: number loci data time points, amount data used classification.
code: # Define a config list quick_config <- list(   n_chains     = 2,   iter         = 1000,   # Increased from 300 to ensure Stan finishes   burn_in_frac = 0.5,   random_seed  = 42 )  # Run the full pipeline results <- MalReBay::MalReBay(   filepath        = input_file_lp,   marker_filepath = marker_file,   mcmc_config     = quick_config,   output_folder   = output_dir_lp,   verbose         = FALSE ) #> Warning in import_data(filepath = filepath, marker_filepath = marker_filepath, #> : NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> Warning in FUN(X[[i]], ...): NAs introduced by coercion #> === validate_stan_data === #>   [PASS] N >= 1 #>   [PASS] J >= 1 #>   [PASS] max_K >= 1 #>   [PASS] length(K) == J #>   [PASS] dim(recoded0) == [N, J*maxMOI] #>   [PASS] additional_counts exists #>   [PASS] additional_counts has data #> === ALL CHECKS PASSED === #> Warning: There were 154 divergent transitions after warmup. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup #> to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them. #> Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems #> Warning: Tail Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior variances and tail quantiles may be unreliable. #> Running the chains for more iterations may help. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess #> === validate_stan_data === #>   [PASS] N >= 1 #>   [PASS] J >= 1 #>   [PASS] max_K >= 1 #>   [PASS] length(K) == J #>   [PASS] dim(recoded0) == [N, J*maxMOI] #>   [PASS] additional_counts exists #>   [PASS] additional_counts has data #> === ALL CHECKS PASSED === #> Warning: There were 169 divergent transitions after warmup. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup #> to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them. #> Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems #> Warning: Bulk Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior means and medians may be unreliable. #> Running the chains for more iterations may help. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#bulk-ess #> Warning: Tail Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior variances and tail quantiles may be unreliable. #> Running the chains for more iterations may help. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess #> === validate_stan_data === #>   [PASS] N >= 1 #>   [PASS] J >= 1 #>   [PASS] max_K >= 1 #>   [PASS] length(K) == J #>   [PASS] dim(recoded0) == [N, J*maxMOI] #>   [PASS] additional_counts exists #>   [PASS] additional_counts has data #> === ALL CHECKS PASSED === #> Warning: There were 193 divergent transitions after warmup. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup #> to find out why this is a problem and how to eliminate them. #> Warning: Examine the pairs() plot to diagnose sampling problems #> Warning: Tail Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior variances and tail quantiles may be unreliable. #> Running the chains for more iterations may help. See #> https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#tail-ess  if (is.null(results)) {   knitr::knit_exit("MCMC failed to produce results; skipping remaining chunks.") } # Access the main results table summary_df <- results$posterior_probabilities  knitr::kable(head(summary_df), caption = "Classification results showing posterior probabilities.")

title: MalReBay
text: histogram posterior probabilities provides clear overview classification results. histogram shows clear separation two groups (one near 0 one near 1), indicates high confidence distinguishing recrudescence reinfection. histogram allows visually assess certainty classifications identify ambiguous cases investigation.  example run, see clear group patients low probability (likely reinfections) another group high probability (likely recrudescences).
code: # Generate the histogram (this is also saved in Step 4 automatically) MalReBay::plot_probability_histogram(results)

title: MalReBay
text: Convergence monitored automatically sampling using log-likelihood standard diagnostics. default, algorithm stop following criteria met: R-hat (Gelman–Rubin diagnostic): monitored parameters must R̂<1.1\hat{R} < 1.1 Gelman–Rubin plot stabilize near 1.0 remain 1.1 threshold. Effective Sample Size (ESS): number effectively independent samples must exceed user-defined threshold (value defined parameter ess_threshold) Maximum iterations: convergence reached, sampling stops iteration cap (value defined parameter max_iterations).

previous_headings: 6. Example of classification: length-polymorphic markers > Step 7: Convergence diagnosis
title: MalReBay
text: MalReBay() function saves MCMC log-likelihood data site within results object. can use exported plot_likelihood_diagnostics function view plots site interactively R session. key plots diagnostics : Trace plot: Chains look like “fuzzy caterpillars” trends. Gelman-Rubin plot: shrink factor approach 1. Histogram: Shows posterior distribution log-likelihood. Autocorrelation (ACF) plot: Correlation drop zero quickly.

previous_headings: 6. Example of classification: length-polymorphic markers > Step 7: Convergence diagnosis
what: Example: Diagnostics for a Single Site
title: MalReBay
text: example using site Benguela, demonstrating generate visualize convergence diagnostics:  trace, Gelman–Rubin, histogram, ACF plots collectively provide visual assurance MCMC chains mixed well stabilized.
code: # Select the first site for diagnostic plotting site_to_plot <- names(results$mcmc_loglikelihoods)[1] loglik_data  <- results$mcmc_loglikelihoods[[site_to_plot]]  if (!is.null(loglik_data)) {   MalReBay::plot_likelihood_diagnostics(     all_chains_loglikelihood = loglik_data,     site_name                = site_to_plot,     stan_fit                 = results$stan_fits[[site_to_plot]],     save_plot                = FALSE,     output_folder            = NULL,     verbose                  = FALSE   ) }

title: MalReBay
text: evaluate performance Bayesian classification MalReBay, compare results simpler match-counting algorithm. algorithm counts allele matches Day 0 recurrence samples provide baseline comparison. length-polymorphic markers, combine outputs MalReBay match-counting algorithm single table. allows us examine, patient: number loci compared number matches samples. per-locus results match-counting (R match, NI non-match, IND indeterminate). posterior probability recrudescence MalReBay (MalReBay_Probability). Comparison MalReBay probabilities traditional match-counting.
code: final_table <- results$comparison  knitr::kable(   head(final_table),    caption = "Comparison of MalReBay probabilities and traditional match-counting.",   digits = 3 ) %>%   kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%")

title: MalReBay

title: MalReBay
text: diagnosing MCMC output, following visual patterns indicate chains may converged: R̂\hat{R} values: Gelman–Rubin plot, values stabilize close 1.0. curve stays well 1.1 drifts without settling, signals non-convergence. Chain mixing: well-mixed traceplots, chains appear overlapping “caterpillars” fluctuate around common mean. traces show different levels, overlap, drift apart, suggests poor mixing. Autocorrelation: good autocorrelation plot shows rapid decline toward zero within lags. autocorrelation remains high decays slowly across iterations, means chains exploring parameter space inefficiently convergence reached.

title: MalReBay
text: chains fail converge, several adjustments can help improve stability mixing: Increase number iterations: Allow sampling time chains better chance explore parameter space thoroughly. Run additional chains parallel: chains improve chances detecting convergence can reveal mixing issues clearly. Adjust chunk size apply thinning: Modifying settings can reduce autocorrelation improve efficiency samples. package also includes automatic convergence check: sampling stop early criteria satisfied, many cases need tune settings manually.

title: MalReBay
text: MalReBay package organized around three main stages: data preparation, Bayesian inference MCMC, summarization results.

title: MalReBay
text: import_data() function reads input Excel file, automatically detects data type (length-polymorphic vs. amplicon sequencing), applies basic cleaning. length-polymorphic markers, define_alleles() groups raw fragment lengths discrete, well-defined allele bins. calculate_frequencies() function estimates initial population-level frequency allele, serves prior MCMC algorithm. recode_alleles() converts allele values integer-based codes, ensuring consistency across sites markers. Output: clean, structured dataset ready passed Bayesian engine.

title: MalReBay
text: core package Gibbs sampling engine explores probability space classifies recurrent infections reinfections recrudescences. run_all_sites() organizes analysis across geographical sites, calling relevant MCMC functions (run_one_chain() run_one_chain_ampseq()). iteration, algorithm updates key parameters: Hidden alleles (switch_hidden_length() / switch_hidden_ampseq()). Population allele frequencies (findposteriorfrequencies()) Multiple chains run parallel, automatic convergence checks based R-hat ESS.

title: MalReBay
text: MCMC converges, package distills results clear, interpretable outputs. Posterior Probabilities: summary table reporting, patient, posterior probability recrudescence. Diagnostic Reports: Functions plot_likelihood_diagnostics() produce trace plots Gelman–Rubin plots convergence checks. Allele Frequency Plots: generate_allele_frequency_plot() visualizes allele distributions overall site. Marker-Level Analysis: Detailed tables show individual genetic markers contribute classification

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: use GitHub flow collaborate project: Fork repository using GitHub interface. Clone fork using git clone fork-url (replace fork-url URL fork). Alternatively, open RStudio IDE create New Project Version Control. Create new branch w/ git checkout -b branch-name (replace branch-name name new branch). Make contribution (see examples). Stage (git add) commit (git commit) changes often necessary Push changes GitHub w/ git push origin branch-name. Submit Pull Request original repo. review PR soon possible.

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: want contribute improving README, please edit README.Rmd (README.md). forget update README.md running:
code: rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: want contribute editing existing vignette, just edit corresponding Rmd file stored vignettes/ folder. want contribute adding new vignette, create new Rmd file vignettes/ folder add following header: use new external dependency, forget add DESCRIPTION file section Suggests (package already listed section Imports). Check integrity package :
code: --- title: "Vignette Title" output: rmarkdown::html_vignette vignette: >   %\VignetteIndexEntry{Vignette Title}   %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}   %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8} --- devtools::check()

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: want contribute improving documentation function, open corresponding file R/ folder edit lines starting #' (roxygen2 syntax). Update documentation (Rd files man/ folder) running: use new external dependency example section, forget add DESCRIPTION file section Imports (package already listed). Check integrity package :
code: devtools::document() devtools::check()

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: want contribute improving code function, open edit corresponding file R/ folder. Check integrity package : forget adapt unit tests function editing corresponding file stored tests/testthat/ folder. use package testthat implement unit tests. Check tests running:
code: devtools::check() devtools::test()

title: Contributing to BayesMalariaTES
text: want contribute submitting new feature, please follow workflow: Create new R file folder R/. Implement code function. Document function w/ roxygen2 syntax. necessary, add additional dependencies DESCRIPTION file. Update package documentation w/ devtools::document(). Create new R file folder tests/testthat/. Implement unit tests new function. Check integrity package w/ devtools::check(). Thanks contribution!

title: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences
text: MalReBay R package Bayesian molecular correction malaria therapeutic efficacy studies (TES). Given paired genotyping data patient’s initial infection (Day 0) later recurrence, estimates posterior probability recurrence recrudescence (treatment failure, parasite persisting) versus reinfection (new parasite acquired treatment). Unlike rule-based allele-matching methods, MalReBay: models sequencing error, allelic dropout, allele loss explicitly handles polyclonal infections (MOI > 1) every locus incorporates local allele frequency information weigh evidence uses full Bayesian MCMC engine (Stan HMC-NUTS) principled uncertainty quantification supports length-polymorphic markers (microsatellites, MSP, GLURP) amplicon sequencing (haplotype) data

title: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences
text: Note: MalReBay links rstan StanHeaders. first use Stan model compiled cached. may take minutes happens per R installation.
code: # Install the development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("SwissTPH/MalReBay")

title: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences
code: library(MalReBay)  # Run the full pipeline on the bundled Angola 2021 TES example data results <- MalReBay(output_folder = "my_results")  # View posterior probabilities head(results$posterior_probabilities)

title: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences
text: MalReBay wraps four-step pipeline can also called step step:
code: import_data()     ↓ classify_infections()     ↓ summarise_results()     ↓ save_results()

what: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC — classify_infections
title: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC — classify_infections
text: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC

title: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC — classify_infections
code: classify_infections(   imported_data,   mcmc_config = system.file("extdata", "default_mcmc_config.xlsx", package = "MalReBay"),   n_workers = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC — classify_infections
text: imported_data List returned import_data(). mcmc_config Path MCMC configuration Excel file. n_workers Parallel workers. Ignored length-polymorphic data (Stan handles parallelism internally). Used ampseq. verbose Print progress messages.

title: Classify malaria infections using Bayesian MCMC — classify_infections
text: List MCMC results passed summarise_results().

what: Import Genotyping Data from Excel — import_data
title: Import Genotyping Data from Excel — import_data
text: Reads data specified Excel file, automatically detecting data type separating sheets structured list.

title: Import Genotyping Data from Excel — import_data
code: import_data(filepath, marker_filepath = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Import Genotyping Data from Excel — import_data
text: filepath full path input Excel file. marker_filepath Path Excel file containing marker metadata (optional marker_info sheet present) verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress data-cleaning messages.

title: Import Genotyping Data from Excel — import_data
text: list containing imported data.

what: MalReBay: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences — MalReBay-package
title: MalReBay: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences — MalReBay-package
text: Classification malaria infection recurrence using Bayesian algorithm implemented Stan efficient sampling.

title: MalReBay: Bayesian classification for malaria recurrences — MalReBay-package
text: Maintainer: Veronica Adhiambo Ochieng veronica.adhiambo@aims.ac.rw Authors: Mateusz Plucinski Monica Golumbeanu monica.golumbeanu@swisstph.ch

what: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
title: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
text: Imports genotype data, runs Bayesian MCMC classification distinguish recrudescent reinfection malaria treatment failures, summarises results, saves output plots tables.

title: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
code: MalReBay(   filepath = system.file("extdata", "Angola_2021_TES_7NMS.xlsx", package = "MalReBay"),   marker_filepath = system.file("extdata", "makers_details.xlsx", package = "MalReBay"),   mcmc_config = system.file("extdata", "default_mcmc_config.xlsx", package = "MalReBay"),   output_folder = NULL,   n_workers = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
text: filepath Path genotype data Excel file. Defaults package example dataset. marker_filepath Path marker metadata Excel file. Defaults package example marker file. mcmc_config Path MCMC configuration Excel file. Defaults package default configuration. output_folder Path folder saving results plots. Set NULL skip saving (results still returned invisibly). n_workers Number parallel workers. length-polymorphic data handled automatically sampler; argument retained compatibility amplicon-sequencing data. verbose TRUE, print progress messages console.

title: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
text: list per-site summary results (invisibly). See summarise_results() details list structure.

title: Run the MalReBay malaria recrudescence classification pipeline — MalReBay
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run on the bundled example data with default settings results <- MalReBay()  # Run on your own data and save output to a folder results <- MalReBay(   filepath      = "path/to/your_data.xlsx",   output_folder = "path/to/output" ) } # }

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: See magrittr::%>% details.

title: Pipe operator — %>%
code: lhs %>% rhs

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: lhs value magrittr placeholder. rhs function call using magrittr semantics.

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: result calling rhs(lhs).

title: Plot MCMC Likelihood Diagnostics — plot_likelihood_diagnostics
text: Calculates standard MCMC convergence diagnostics generates diagnostic plots multi-stage process: 1) Gelman-Rubin, 2) Traceplot & Histogram, 3) ACF plots.

title: Plot MCMC Likelihood Diagnostics — plot_likelihood_diagnostics
code: plot_likelihood_diagnostics(   all_chains_loglikelihood = NULL,   site_name,   stan_fit = NULL,   save_plot = TRUE,   output_folder = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot MCMC Likelihood Diagnostics — plot_likelihood_diagnostics
text: all_chains_loglikelihood list element numeric vector representing log-likelihood history one MCMC chain. site_name character string labeling plots. stan_fit optional stanfit object (rstan) additional diagnostics. save_plot logical. TRUE, plots saved PNG files. output_folder character string specifying path save plots. verbose logical. TRUE, prints diagnostic summaries.

title: Plot MCMC Likelihood Diagnostics — plot_likelihood_diagnostics
text: invisible list containing calculated diagnostic results.

what: Generate and Save Diversity Pie Charts — plot_markers_diversity
title: Generate and Save Diversity Pie Charts — plot_markers_diversity
text: Creates pie charts visualising allele haplotype diversity across samples combined (Day 0 recurrences pooled). Pies arranged max_cols per row.

title: Generate and Save Diversity Pie Charts — plot_markers_diversity
code: plot_markers_diversity(   genotypedata,   data_type,   marker_info = NULL,   output_folder = NULL,   filename_prefix = "diversity",   max_cols = 4 )

title: Generate and Save Diversity Pie Charts — plot_markers_diversity
text: genotypedata dataframe containing genotyping data. data_type string: "length_polymorphic" "ampseq". marker_info dataframe marker definitions; required data_type = "length_polymorphic". output_folder Path directory output PNG saved. filename_prefix string prefix output filename. max_cols Maximum number pie charts per row. Defaults 4.

title: Generate and Save Diversity Pie Charts — plot_markers_diversity
text: Invisibly returns combined ggplot object, NULL data.

title: Plot Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) — plot_moi
text: function calculates MOI (number distinct alleles per marker per sample) generates one violin plot per site improved readability.

title: Plot Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) — plot_moi
code: plot_moi(   genotypedata,   marker_pattern = "(_allele_\\d+|_\\d+)$",   output_folder = NULL,   filename_prefix = "moi_per_marker" )

title: Plot Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) — plot_moi
text: genotypedata data frame containing Sample.ID, Site, marker columns. marker_pattern regex pattern identify marker columns. Defaults standard allele suffix patterns. output_folder Path directory plots saved. NULL, plots saved disk. filename_prefix Prefix output PNG filenames. file named <prefix>_<site>.png.

title: Plot Multiplicity of Infection (MOI) — plot_moi
text: named list ggplot objects, one per site. underlying MOI data attached plot attribute "moi_data".

title: Plot Posterior Probability Histogram — plot_probability_histogram
text: Creates saves histogram posterior probabilities recrudescence patients. internal function called automatically MalReBay.

title: Plot Posterior Probability Histogram — plot_probability_histogram
code: plot_probability_histogram(   summary_results,   output_folder = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot Posterior Probability Histogram — plot_probability_histogram
text: summary_results list returned summarise_results, containing posterior_probabilities data frame Probability column. output_folder string specifying directory histogram PNG saved. Defaults "results". verbose Logical. TRUE, prints message file saved. Defaults TRUE.

title: Plot Posterior Probability Histogram — plot_probability_histogram
text: output_folder NULL, displays histogram current graphics device returns invisible(NULL). output_folder provided, saves PNG returns file path invisibly. Returns invisible(NULL) probabilities available.

what: Save Classification Results and Generate Plots — save_results
title: Save Classification Results and Generate Plots — save_results
text: Writes MCMC results CSV files generates descriptive result-based plots (Diversity, MOI, Posterior Histograms).

title: Save Classification Results and Generate Plots — save_results
code: save_results(   summary_results,   imported_data = NULL,   output_folder = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Save Classification Results and Generate Plots — save_results
text: summary_results List summarise_results. imported_data List import_data. NULL, data-dependent plots (diversity, MOI) skipped. output_folder Path save files. NULL prints plots R plot window skips CSV saving. verbose Logical.

title: Save Classification Results and Generate Plots — save_results
text: named character vector paths files written, invisible(NULL) output_folder NULL.

title: Summarise MCMC Classification Results — summarise_results
text: Post-processes raw MCMC output classify_infections interpretable summaries including posterior probabilities, convergence diagnostics, match counting comparison table. Called automatically MalReBay.

title: Summarise MCMC Classification Results — summarise_results
code: summarise_results(   mcmc_results,   imported_data,   output_folder = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Summarise MCMC Classification Results — summarise_results
text: mcmc_results list returned classify_infections. imported_data list returned import_data. output_folder Path saving convergence diagnostic plots. NULL skips saving. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Summarise MCMC Classification Results — summarise_results
text: named list posterior_probabilities, comparison, convergence, mcmc_loglikelihoods.

title: MalReBay 0.0.1
text: first release version MalReBay! 🎉 Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Functions: MalReBay() import_data() plot_likelihood_diagnostics() plot_probability_histogram() plot_moi() plot_markers_diversity() Example data: Angola_2021_TES_7NMS.xlsx — genotyping data Therapeutic Efficacy Study conducted Angola 2021. Contains 70 patients three sites (Benguela, Lunda Sul, Zaire) genotyped 7 microsatellite markers. Amplicon_Sequencing.xlsx — example amplicon sequencing dataset demonstrating ampseq workflow. makers_details.xlsx — marker metadata file containing marker IDs, binning methods, repeat lengths required import_data(). default_mcmc_config.xlsx — default MCMC configuration file recommended settings n_chains, iter, burn_in_frac, adapt_delta, random_seed. files can accessed via: Vignettes: Getting started
code: system.file("extdata", "filename.xlsx", package = "MalReBay")
